text: fat is 0.1g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, glycemic behaviour matters a ton.
carbs is 6.4g. glycemic index, fibre, and starch are doing most of the work. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
protein is 0.9g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin c at 23% DV, vitamin a at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side. practical staple carb source because tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day. whole-food carb package because that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs. can be quite filling because a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

on the weaker side. still a carb-heavy category because that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage. preparation changes outcomes a lot because what you do to a tuber can make or break it. protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.
C tier. Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
